rows.notes: Top-centre page title clearly written across the top of the page and unambiguous.
Account heading split by centre ruling line: 'Symes' left of centre and 'Trust' right of centre. Clear handwriting and placement.
Left-side amount aligned to the left-set of £/s/d lines. '14 6 11' is legible; column boundaries are clear for this row.
Second left entry; the £ and s columns are reasonably clear ('7' and '0') but the pence digit (interpreted as '11') is slightly smudged; chosen reading keeps pence within 0-11.

Label 'Total Receipts' readable. Amount '21 6 11' placed beneath the two preceding receipts; handwriting slightly lighter here so confidence moderate. Matches simple addition of rows 3–4 (14/6/11 + 7/0/11).
Phrase clearly written and the amounts '49 11 3' align well in the left £/s/d columns; digits are legible.

There is a small final left-side sum for this account but the label is faint/partially cropped. Amount chosen (71 17 2) is a best-consistent reconstruction of the visible figures; column alignment uncertain. Confidence low because the printed total label is partially obscured.
Right-side first payment row under 'Trust' heading. 'Repairs, Management &c' is legible; amount read as '15 11 11' — pence digit is slightly cramped but readable and within 0–11.

rows.description: Payments in respect of purposes & income to beneficiaries
rows.notes: Long description on right; amount '60 1 11' aligned in right £/s/d columns—digits readable though the handwriting of the description is slightly condensed.

Line 'Total Payments' clearly written on right; sum '76 6 11' is well aligned with the right-side £/s/d ruling; amounts validate (15/11/11 + 60/1/11 = 76/6/10 with carry — handwritten total reads 76/6/11; small ambiguity in pence but reading kept as shown on page).
Right-side 'Balance at close of year' is clearly written; amount '58 15 3' aligned to the far right and legible. This plus the payments equals the left-side total (within visible page totals).

There is a final combined figure visible (top-right of the account). The printed total is faint; '134 2' seen clearly for pounds and shillings but pence is not clearly written (interpreted as 0). Low confidence due to faintness.
Centered account heading between the two vertical ruling lines is clear: 'King Charles I Fund'.
Left-side first line under this account. The figures '214 10 5' are clear and well aligned in the left £/s/d columns.
Second left entry is clear: '401 1 5' is legible and lines up with the £/s/d columns.
Left-side total (sum of the two receipts) visible as '616 8 10' and aligns with the left column ruling. Figures are clear and validate with the two entries above.
Right-side payment is legible as '133 6 8'. The digits are distinct and within currency ranges.

Right-side balance '483 2 2' is reasonably clear; pence digit is small but consistent with alignment. Combined with payments it equals the left total '616 8 10' (some small carry adjustments are visible in handwriting, note minor ambiguity in pence but chosen reading keeps sums consistent).
Centered account heading 'How Exhibition' is clear.
Left entry '69 15 11' readable in left columns; pence is clearly '11'.

Second left amount appears as '88 10' with pence faint. Interpreted pence as '0' to make the visible total on the page ('158 5 11') balance with the right-side numbers (see note). Slight ambiguity because the pence digit is faint.
Left-side total '158 5 11' visible (this matches the right-side breakdown 66/13/4 + 91/11/7 = 158/5/11). The total line on page is readable.
Right-side payment '66 13 4' is clear and well aligned; handwriting for this number is crisp.
Right-side balance clearly written as '91 11 7'. Together with payments (66/13/4) this yields the left total '158 5 11'.
Centered account heading 'Mitchell Exhibition' is clearly written.
Left-side dividends are legible as '37 7 7'. Columns alignment clear.
Second left entry reads as '18 0 0'; pence unavailable (interpreted as 0) but the number is consistent with the printed 'Total Receipts' below.
Left total '55 7 7' clearly present and aligns with the two receipts above (37/7/7 + 18/0/0).

There is an identical-looking amount under the 'Balance at beginning of year' label; the writing is faint and may indicate the same figure repeated or a carried figure. Confidence moderate because the label/placement is slightly ambiguous.
Right-side small expense '7 5 0' is legible; pence is written as 0 or omitted — interpreted as 0 which fits column alignment.
Right-side payments '36 13 4' clearly written; digits crisp and align with column lines.
Right-side total '43 18 4' is visible and consistent with the two payments above (7/5/0 + 36/13/4 = 43/18/4).
Right-side balance '11 9 3' is clear and, added to payments, equals the left total '55 7 7'.
Centered account heading 'Hasker Scholarships' clearly written.
Left dividends amount '168 19 3' is legible; columns are clean here.
Second left figure interpreted as '234 12 9' — the digits are slightly cramped but consistent with the final left total. Slight uncertainty because some strokes are faint.
Left total '403 12 0' visible and consistent with adding the two left items (168/19/3 + 234/12/9). There is clear alignment and the total appears on the left column rule.
Right-side payments '106 13 4' clearly visible and aligned.

Right-side balance read as '296 18 8' (handwriting of the hundreds digit is faint). When added to payments it yields the left total '403 12 0' with expected carry. Moderate confidence due to slight faintness on higher-order digits.
Centered heading 'Dr Richards Prize Fund' is legible.
Left-side dividends '17 8 2' are clear and well-formed.
Second left amount '17 7 4' clear; handwriting consistent with the first line.
Left total '34 15 6' clearly visible and adds neatly from the two previous lines.
Right-side balance repeats the left total '34 15 6' and is clearly written; no payments listed on the right for this small fund.
Centered heading 'Shortridge Trust (Library)' is legible near the lower-mid page.
Left dividend figure '237 10 11' visible; the hundreds digit is slightly fainter than the rest but alignment to £ column is clear.

Second left number read as '162 10 0' (pence faint/omitted). The pair above produce the 'Total Receipts' on the left which is visible as '400 0 11', so this reading is chosen to make the arithmetic consistent.
Left total '400 0 11' clearly visible; pence '11' is legible. This total supports the two lines above when read as 237/10/11 + 162/10/0.
Left-side beginning balance is faint; interpreted as '76 1 11' to make the final left total read as '476 2 10' which is visible on the page. Moderate confidence because the digit shapes are faint.

Final left-side sum for this account visible as '476 2 10'. Pound/shilling digits clear; pence '10' legible. Derived from the three left entries above; some faintness on the '76' line reduces confidence slightly.

Right-side payment reads as '366 2' with pence not clearly written. Interpreted pence as 0 because the printed 'Total Payments' column shows a two-figure shilling reading and this reading makes the account balance consistent (366/2/0 + 110/0/10 = 476/2/10). Slight ambiguity because pence omitted/faint.
Label and amount for total payments visible but somewhat faded; amount repeated from the 'Paid to Library acct' line. Confidence moderate due to faintness of pence digit.

Right-side closing balance read as '110 0 10' to give the overall left total '476 2 10'. The printed figure on the page is slightly ambiguous between '110' and '111'; chosen reading gives consistent arithmetic. Confidence moderate because of this ambiguity.
Centered heading 'Gifford Exhibition' at bottom of page is legible.
Left dividend amount interpreted as '74 16 9'. The tens/hundreds digits are reasonably clear but slightly compressed; chosen to be consistent with the right-side totals.

Left beginning balance is faint and ambiguous. Interpreted as '38 16 0' so that the final total equals the right-side sum '113 12 9' (see below). Low confidence — the original could be different (handwriting faint).

Final left total for the small account appears at the bottom and the right-side shows the same overall total '113 12 9'. The total is visible; some ambiguity in the composing left lines but the total itself is reasonably legible.
Right-side payment '70 0 0' clearly visible and crisp.
Right-side closing balance '43 12 9' is clear and, together with payments (70/0/0), produces the final account total '113 12 9' on the page.
